decision: Slow-mo powerup drives Rapier via manual stepping: <Physics paused> + a single PhysicsStepDriver useFrame(priority -10) calling rapier.step(realDt * timeScale()).
why: @react-three/rapier's fixed-step accumulator advances sim-time 1:1 with real-time regardless of the timeStep prop (cadence threshold == integration dt, ratio fixed at 1; stepWorld overwrites world.timestep each step so external override is futile). The exposed useRapier().step(dt) is the SAME function the auto-loop uses (accumulator, event queue, sensors, interpolation all intact), so passing a scaled dt is genuine bullet-time with no behavioral loss.

decision: Score-doubler powerup doubles CRYSTAL value while active (scoreMultiplier() in powerupBridge, applied at CrystalField collect), not a final-score multiplier.
why: computeScore is a pure function of run TOTALS (height monotonic, crystals discrete, maxCombo a max) computed at run end — there is no running score counter to multiply during a window. Crystals are the only discrete, collectible score source that maps cleanly to a timed multiplier, so the doubler doubles each gem's value for its duration.

decision: Multi-bounce powerup is a CHARGE STACK (bounceCharges count, not a timer): grants 3 free mid-air bounces, each spent by an airborne TAP (useDrag tap flag) → requestMidAirBounce → PlayerBlob pops the blob to +22 vy. Pickups stack additively.
why: A timed mid-air-bounce buff is awkward (you'd waste it if no bounce was needed in the window); a discrete charge stack lets the player save the recovery for when they actually mis-arc. Tap (vs drag) cleanly separates it from the existing mid-air steering drag with no new input surface.

decision: Folded final pre-PR review: (HIGH) GooCsg refraction uResolution refreshed per-frame from live fbo.width/height since drei useFBO resizes in place (same identity) so an [fbo]-keyed effect goes stale on window resize; (MED) the FBO backbuffer pass now force-clears with autoClear save/restore so EffectComposer's managed autoClear can't ghost it; (simplifier) powerupBridge TIMED_POWERUPS single source of truth; (security) itch-library.mjs .env existsSync guard.
why: Definition-of-done milestone-boundary review before the single push+PR; fold findings forward, never leave a known HIGH bug.

decision: Introduce data-driven biomePropRegistry keyed by canonical biome band name (from src/config/biomes.json), replacing the 4 hardcoded model components and the drifted y<160/500/900 thresholds in BiomeScenicProps
why: 6 canonical bands exist in biomes.json (ground/sky/upper-atmosphere/stratosphere/space/deep-space) but BiomeScenicProps invented its own 4-band split with magic numbers — single source of truth + extensibility (add a prop = add data, not a React component + 4 edits) + richer per-band variety

decision: Per-biome ambience is a DATA EXTENSION of BiomeProps, not a new layer: add a biomeAmbience config table (per-band mote color + opacity) keyed off biomeBandAt, replacing the hardcoded moteColor() h<200/h<650 thresholds
why: BiomeProps.moteColor uses the same drifted-magic-number anti-pattern just fixed for scenery; the canonical 6-band logic (biomeBandAt) is the single source of truth and ambience should share it. Motes already exist + are cheap — recoloring per band via config is the minimal correct change, no new render layer needed

decision: Next milestone after PR #59 merge: align ambient audio to canonical biome bands. audioCfg.ambientBands has its own drifted 4-band split (0/200/600/950) that mismatches the 6 canonical bands and only covers 4 of 6 (missing upper-atmosphere + deep-space). Refactor ambientBandFor() in howler.ts to use biomeBandAt, and map an ambient bed for all 6 bands (reuse nearest existing mp3 where no dedicated bed, OR source new beds via itch pipeline)
why: Same drift anti-pattern fixed for scenery + motes; biomeBandAt is the single source of truth. Completes the 3rd sensory dimension of biome identity (visual scenery + motes done, audio is the gap). On a FRESH branch after #59 merges

decision: Replace audioCfg.ambientBands (drifted 0/200/600/950 4-band split) with biomeBandAt as the ambient band resolver, and map an ambient bed for ALL 6 canonical bands explicitly in audio.json: ground→forest, sky→wind, upper-atmosphere→wind, stratosphere→strongwind, space→space, deep-space→space. setAmbientBand's silent `if(!path)return` becomes a loud error for an unmapped band (no-fallbacks)
why: audio is the 3rd sensory dimension of biome identity (scenery + motes already aligned in PR#59); ambient drift (4 bands, own thresholds) mismatched the canonical 6. Reuse the 4 existing beds explicitly across 6 bands rather than leaving 2 bands silent. biomeBandAt is the single source of truth

subject: feat(audio): align ambient beds to the canonical biome bands (#61)

decision: Parallax = discrete depth layers as a data-driven extension of the scenery system. Add a far backdrop silhouette layer (z ~−60..−40, large slow-drift, taller wrap column → reads as distant) behind the existing BiomeScenicProps (the mid layer), plus optional near foreground accents (z ~−5..+2, fast drift). Each layer: z-range, drift multiplier, scale multiplier, density, wrap-column height. Far layers reuse biome prop GLBs at large scale as silhouettes (or simple shapes); biome-band-aware via biomeBandAt
why: True depth parallax needs ≥2 layers drifting at depth-scaled rates; the existing scenery is one plane (z −26..−10) so the world reads flat. Reuse the registry + biomeBandAt (single source of truth) + wrap logic rather than a new system. Discrete layers are simpler + more controllable than per-instance depth-continuous drift

decision: Per-biome particles EXTEND the existing mote ambience layer (not a new layer): add size + drift + opacity already there; per-band the motes vary in size scale, drift speed, and recolor — driven by the existing biomeAmbience config + biomeAmbienceAt. Larger slow warm dust on ground → tiny fast cosmic shimmer up high. Reuse the single cheap instanced mote mesh; per-instance size/drift read from band config
why: the mote layer already exists, is cheap (one instanced mesh), and is recolored per band via biomeAmbienceAt. Extending it with per-band size/drift is the minimal correct change that completes the sensory arc; a whole new instanced particle layer would add draw cost for marginal gain. biomeAmbienceAt stays the single source of truth

decision: Teleport via the same consume-bridge pattern as launch: requestTeleport(y)/consumeTeleport() in launchBridge; PlayerBlob frame loop consumes it → ensureHeight(y) to extend world-gen there, body.setTranslation to (0,y,0), setLinvel zero, wakeUp, sync maxY/safeY/prevY/lastEnsureY refs + reset dead flag. Exposed via DevHarness per-band buttons + window.__blobtest.teleport(y). Dev/test only
why: matches the established launch/nudge/gate consume pattern; ensureHeight already extends world-gen so pads+scenery exist at the target; zeroing velocity + waking + syncing the death/safe refs keeps the sim consistent (a teleport mid-fall must not instantly read as a death). Reuses the test bridge for E2E + lets me screenshot-verify every band

decision: Add ACHIEVEMENT_SKIN map (achievement id -> BlobSkin) in src/sim/achievements. checkAndUnlock (pure) merges any fresh achievement's tied skin into unlockedSkins in the returned progress. Make 2 skins ACHIEVEMENT-EXCLUSIVE (ghost via score-25k, ink via height-1000) — removed from the crystal-buyable path so the achievement is the only way; blue (starter) + slime stay crystal-buyable. Customizer shows achievement-gated tiles with 'Earn: <achievement title>' instead of a crystal price
why: ties the existing achievements + skins systems into a progression reward the toast already hints at, adding depth/fun without new assets. Keeping the grant inside the pure checkAndUnlock means it persists + flows through the same path as the achievement unlock itself. Exclusive (not also-buyable) makes the achievement meaningful

decision: Treasure is a NEW TOP CRYSTAL TIER ('treasure'), not a new collectible type. Add to CrystalTier union: value ~25, scale ~2.4, very-rare odds (ramps ~0.5%→3% with altitude), distinct golden color + extra glint VFX. CrystalField renders a treasure GLB (chest/big gem from NAS) when tier==treasure, gem geometry otherwise. Collection fires a celebratory flash + a stinger (reuse milestone/record stinger or a new coin-jackpot). Reuses the whole crystal spawn/collect/magnet/render pipeline — minimal new surface
why: the crystal tier system already exists with per-tier value/scale/odds + render; adding a 4th rarer tier reuses spawn/collect/magnet/HUD/score with no new bridge or pipeline. A separate collectible type would duplicate all of that for marginal gain. Fits the established data-driven pattern

decision: BiomeBanner mirrors DifficultyBanner but with a SOFT cue (brief blue flash + playChime, NOT the difficulty-up gold-flash/playMilestone — that banner owns the loud moment). Friendly labels via a biomeBandLabel map in biomes.ts (single source of truth): ground→'The Ground', sky→'The Sky', upper-atmosphere→'Upper Atmosphere', stratosphere→'The Stratosphere', space→'Space', deep-space→'Deep Space'. Fire ONLY on an upward band crossing (band index increases), gated PLAYING. Watches run.height like DifficultyBanner
why: reuses the established banner pattern + biomeBandAt (single source of truth); a softer cue avoids competing with the difficulty banner's loud transition. Player-facing payoff for the four-dimension biome work. No new assets
